data.title: New Fortress Energy Closes $266 Million Turbine Sale-Leaseback With Macquarie; 10-Year Term
data.description:
New Fortress Energy completed a turbine sale-leaseback transaction with Macquarie for $265.9 million. On Apr 1, 2026, the company sold the equipment and entered into a 10-year master lease expected to begin Jul 1, 2026. The company provided a parent guarantee of the lessee's obligations. Net proceeds were used to repay certain indebtedness, improving liquidity.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Turbine sale-leaseback (Asset Purchase Agreement and 10-year Master Lease)
  • Counterparty: Macquarie Energy
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 01 2026 / Jul 01 2026
  • Duration / Termination: 10 years
  • Reason: Repay debt and improve liquidity

Original SEC Filing: New Fortress Energy Inc. [ NFE ] - 8-K - Apr. 07, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Nabors Industries Raises Letter of Credit Capacity to $150 Million Under Citi-Led Agreement
data.description:
Nabors Industries entered into an Incremental Joinder to its amended and restated credit agreement to expand letter of credit capacity. The change increases the Letters of Credit Maximum Amount by $25 million to $150 million with Citibank as administrative agent and Bank of Texas as the incremental participant. The adjustment allows Nabors Delaware to issue reimbursement obligations under letters of credit up to $150 million outstanding and does not reduce revolving loan capacity. The move enhances liquidity support for operations and projects.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Incremental joinder increasing letter of credit facility under amended and restated credit agreement
  • Counterparty: Citibank, as administrative agent; Bank of Texas; issuing banks
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 07 2026 / Apr 07 2026
  • Reason: Expand LC capacity for operational and project needs

Original SEC Filing: NABORS INDUSTRIES LTD [ NBR ] - 8-K - Apr. 07, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Grown Rogue Reports $32.4M Revenue, $3.2M GAAP Net Income for 2025
data.description:
Grown Rogue reported preliminary, unaudited 2025 results showing revenue of $32.4 million and GAAP net income of $3.2 million, following its transition from IFRS to U.S. GAAP and consolidation of New Jersey operations. Adjusted EBITDA (non-GAAP) rose to $5.4 million for the year, and the company ended 2025 with $11.4 million of cash. Management provided 2026 guidance and a multi-year growth framework targeting material capacity expansions and higher branded packaged product mix.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue (GAAP) for full year 2025: $32.4 million (up 22% vs. 2024).
  • Gross profit for 2025: $14.15 million (as reported).
  • GAAP net income for 2025: $3.23 million (includes non-cash gains related to derivative liabilities).
  • Adjusted EBITDA (non-GAAP) for 2025: $5.39 million (16.5% margin reported).
  • Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2025: $11.37 million.

Business Highlights

  • New Jersey consolidation: ABCO Garden State contributed materially in its first full year, with full sell-through of packaged, branded products and an ongoing Phase II expansion to reach ~16,000 sq ft flowering canopy by incremental room completions through 2026.
  • Market dynamics: Michigan and Oregon experienced significant pricing pressure in 2025, contributing to YoY revenue declines in those markets despite cost-of-production improvements (indoor biomass cost noted below $225/lb in mature facilities overall).
  • Capacity and expansion roadmap: Company operated ~37,000 sq ft of flowering canopy across three states at year-end and expects to reach ~58,000 sq ft by end of 2026, with new-build cultivation in Minnesota (Phase I ~8,000 sq ft targeted online late Q3 2026) and an Illinois turnkey facility planned to start contributing in Q4 2026.
  • Segment performance: 2025 revenue by state showed New Jersey ~$11.3M (vs. $0.3M in 2024), Oregon ~$11.1M, and Michigan ~$10.0M, reflecting the late-2024 entry and ramp in New Jersey.
  • Capital and funding: Closed $12.0 million of senior secured credit facility tranches in 2025 (blended interest ~7.84%); 2026 guidance excludes estimated pre-revenue startup costs for Illinois and Minnesota and plans to fund mid-2027 expansions with internal cash flow and existing capital.

Original SEC Filing: Grown Rogue International Inc. [ GRUSF ] - 8-K - Apr. 07, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Genie Energy Received Notice of Noncompliance
data.description:
On April 1, 2026, the company received a notice from the NYSE that it is not in compliance with Section 802.01E due to the delayed filing of its 2025 Form 10-K. The delay stems from the need to restate audited financial statements for 2024 and 2023.

Original SEC Filing: Genie Energy Ltd. [ GNE ] - 8-K - Apr. 07, 2026

data.title: Flowers Foods Sets $400 Million Term Loan, Extends Revolver Covenant Holiday to Oct 2027
data.description:
Flowers Foods executed two financing actions to bolster liquidity and manage upcoming maturities. The company arranged a $400 million senior unsecured delayed-draw term loan to refinance its 3.500% notes due October 2026, with availability through Oct. 1, 2026 and a three-year maturity from funding. It also amended its revolving credit facility to extend the Covenant Holiday through the fiscal quarter ending Oct. 9, 2027, add a lower-ratings pricing tier, and align terms with the new term loan. Together, these steps are intended to enhance financial flexibility and streamline covenant frameworks.

Agreement 1: Flowers Foods Arranges $400 Million Delayed-Draw Term Loan to Refinance 2026 Notes

  • Agreement type: Senior unsecured delayed-draw term loan credit facility
  • Counterparty: Wells Fargo Bank, as Administrative Agent, and other lenders
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 6 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: 3 years from funding date
  • Reason: Refinance 2026 notes and enhance financial flexibility

Agreement 2: Flowers Foods Amends Revolving Credit Facility, Extends Covenant Holiday to Oct. 2027

  • Agreement type: First Amendment to revolving credit facility
  • Counterparty: Wells Fargo Bank, as Administrative Agent, and other lenders
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 6 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Through Oct 9 2027 (Covenant Holiday)
  • Reason: Align revolver with term loan and extend covenant relief

Original SEC Filing: FLOWERS FOODS INC [ FLO ] - 8-K - Apr. 07, 2026

Disclaimer

data.description:

data.title: American Healthcare REIT Secures Amended Credit Facility: $800M Revolver to 2030, $550M Term Loan
data.description:
American Healthcare REIT entered into a Second Amendment to its Credit Agreement, establishing an amended $800 million senior unsecured revolving facility maturing in 2030 with two six-month extension options and a $550 million senior unsecured term loan maturing in 2027. The facilities bear interest based on Daily Simple SOFR or Term SOFR plus an applicable margin. The company may increase the facilities so that total term loans and available revolver commitments do not exceed $1.85 billion, subject to conditions. Aggregate borrowing capacity under the amended facility was $1.35 billion as of April 1, 2026.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Amended senior unsecured revolving and term loan credit facility
  • Counterparty: Bank of America, as administrative agent, and a syndicate of lenders
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 01 2026 / Apr 01 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Revolver matures Apr 1 2030 with two 6-month extensions; term loan matures Jan 19 2027
  • Reason: Refinance, extend maturities, and enhance liquidity

Original SEC Filing: American Healthcare REIT, Inc. [ AHR ] - 8-K - Apr. 07, 2026

Disclaimer

All prices as of 2038 GMT

EQUITIES

GLOBAL - MSCI's global equities gauge fell slightly while the dollar and U.S. Treasuries were little changed on Tuesday as investors awaited the outcome of a standoff between the U.S. and Iran.

MSCI's gauge of stocks across the globe EURONEXT:IACWI fell 3.48 points, or 0.35%, to 994.18.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

NEW YORK - U.S. stocks ended mixed on Tuesday amid signs of progress in negotiations as the minutes ticked down to President Donald Trump's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz.

According to preliminary data, the S&P 500 gained 6.09 points, or 0.09%, to end at 6,617.92 points, while the Nasdaq Composite gained 28.46 points, or 0.13%, to 22,024.80. The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 78.54 points, or 0.17%, to 46,591.34.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

LONDON - European shares retreated on Tuesday, led by declines in defence and healthcare stocks, as investors turned cautious ahead of U.S. President Donald Trump’s deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

The STOXX 600 index ended down 1% at 590.59, giving up earlier gains.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

TOKYO - The Nikkei share average ended flat in choppy trade on Tuesday, as investors balanced optimism over a potential Middle East ceasefire and unease over U.S. President Donald Trump's threat to escalate strikes on Iran if it fails to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

Nikkei swung between gains and losses before closing 0.03% higher at 53,429.56.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SHANGHAI - China stocks traded steady on Tuesday as markets remain focused on the war in the Middle East and high oil prices, whose impact on China has so far been relatively limited.

The blue-chip CSI300 Index closed flat.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

AUSTRALIA - Australian shares were set to open little changed on Wednesday, as strength in commodity prices could underpin miners, but gains were likely to be limited on investor caution ahead of a looming U.S. deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a flashpoint that has kept markets on edge.

The local share price index futures (YAPcm1) rose 0.02%, a 35.2-point premium to the underlying S&P/ASX 200 index close. The benchmark rose 1.7% on Tuesday.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SEOUL - South Korean shares closed higher on Tuesday, led by Samsung Electronics after the world's largest memory chipmaker flagged an eight-fold jump in quarterly profit. The won strengthened, while the benchmark bond yield rose.

The benchmark KOSPI . (KS11) rose 44.44 points, or 0.82%, to 5,494.77.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

FOREIGN EXCHANGE

NEW YORK - The U.S. dollar traded close to its highest levels in almost 11 months on Tuesday with investors pausing ahead of a U.S.-imposed deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to shipping or face attacks on its infrastructure.

The U.S. dollar index was at 99.852. It hit 100.64 last week, its highest since May 2025.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

CHINA - China's yuan jumped about 0.4% against the dollar on Tuesday, hitting the strongest level in five weeks, with traders expecting increasing volatility driven by the Iran war and shaky China-U.S. trade relations.

The onshore yuan changed hands at 6.8587 per dollar at 0941 GMT, 0.4% firmer than the previous session's close and on track for its best day in a month.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

AUSTRALIA - The Australian and New Zealand dollars eased on Tuesday as investors anxiously counted down to the latest U.S. deadline on threats to strike Iranian infrastructure, with any escalation likely to sap confidence just as New Zealand's central bank meets.

That saw the Aussie slip back 0.2% to $0.6902 , having firmed 0.5% overnight. Support lies at the recent 10-week low of $0.6834 with resistance around $0.6964.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SEOUL - The Korean won strengthened against dollar on tuesday.

The won was quoted at 1,504.2 per dollar on the onshore settlement platform , 0.37% stronger than its previous close at 1,509.8.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

TREASURIES

NEW YORK - U.S. Treasury yields were little changed in choppy trading on Tuesday as a deadline set by U.S. President Donald Trump for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz drew closer with little sign an agreement could be reached.

The yield on the benchmark U.S. 10-year Treasury note (US10YT=TWEB) edged up 0.6 basis point to 4.341% after earlier reaching a session high of 4.38%.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

LONDON - Euro zone bond yields surged on Tuesday as uncertainty over the Iran war persisted and a U.S.-imposed deadline for a deal on the conflict loomed large.

The yield on the benchmark German 10-year Bund (DE10YT=RR) was last up almost 7 basis points at 3.063%.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

TOKYO - Japanese government bonds (JGBs) advanced on Tuesday after a smooth 30-year bond auction reassured investors, easing market concerns that demand for the sale might be weak.

The 30-year JGB yield (JP30YTN=JBTC) sank 2 basis points (bps) to 3.735%. Yields move inversely to bond prices.

For a full report, click on

COMMODITIES

GOLD

Gold prices drifted higher on Tuesday, with market attention on U.S. President Donald Trump's looming deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or risk devastating attacks on its infrastructure.

Spot gold was up 0.8% at $4,684.59 per ounce by 2:33 p.m. ET (1833 GMT).

For a full report, click on

- - - -

IRON ORE

Iron ore futures rose for the first time in three sessions on Tuesday as trading resumed in top buyer China after a long holiday weekend, with investors awaiting clarity on the prospects of a resolution to the Iran war.

The most-traded May iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) climbed 0.5% to 805 yuan ($117.03) a metric ton by 0243 GMT.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

BASE METALS

Aluminium prices rose on Tuesday with the market pricing in confirmation of prolonged repairs which a smelter in the UAE is facing after an Iranian attack late last month.

The three-month aluminium contract on the London Metal Exchange (LME) gained 0.1% to $3,472.50 a metric ton by 1600 GMT.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

OIL

Oil prices settled mixed on Tuesday, with Brent down on worries that high energy prices could slow economic growth, while U.S. crude closed at its highest since 2022 ahead of U.S. President Donald Trump's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz.

Brent futures fell 50 cents, or 0.5%, to settle at $109.27 a barrel. U.S. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude rose 54 cents, or 0.5%, to settle at $112.95, well off its session high when it was up by over $5 a barrel.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

PALM OIL

Malaysian palm oil futures settled lower for a second straight session on Tuesday, erasing intraday gains, as weaker crude oil prices weighed on the market.

The benchmark palm oil contract for June delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange shed 45 ringgit, or 0.94%, to 4,766 ringgit ($1,183.22) a metric ton at the close.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

RUBBER

Japanese rubber futures rose for a fifth straight session on Tuesday to a one-and-a-half-year high, tracking stronger oil prices, while focus was also on the developments in the Middle East as the deadline for a ceasefire deal approached.

The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for September delivery , was up 3 yen, or 0.76%, at 397.4 yen ($2.49) per kg.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

data.title: Phoenix Education Partners Gets $50M Stock Buyback Authorization, Posts Lower 2Q Profit
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Phoenix Education Partners said its board authorized a stock buyback program for up to $50 million.

The parent company of the University of Phoenix also reported a second-quarter profit of $10.8 million, or 28 cents a share, down from $16.1 million, or 43 cents a share, a year earlier.

Stripping out certain one-time items, adjusted earnings were 58 cents a share. Analysts polled by FactSet were expecting 34 cents a share.

Net revenue fell to $222.5 million, down from $223.4 million. Analysts were expecting $220.3 million.

For the current fiscal year, the company expects net revenue between $1.03 billion and $1.04 billion, with adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization of between $244 million and $249 million.

Shares rose 8.3% to $33.60 in after-hours trading on Tuesday.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Innospec Schedules First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release and Conference Call
data.description:
ENGLEWOOD, Colo., April 07, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Innospec Inc. today announced that it will release first quarter 2026 earnings results on Thursday, May 7, 2026 after market close. Following the release of its results, Patrick S. Williams, President and Chief Executive Officer, and Ian Cleminson, Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, will host an interactive conference call on Friday, May 8, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. ET.

The public is invited to listen to the conference call by registering in advance using the below Online Registration Link. Upon registering, each participant will receive an email confirmation with dial-in numbers and a unique Personal PIN which can be used to join the conference 5-10 minutes prior to the start time.

Online Registration Link https://register-conf.media-server.com/register/BI0c6aeeb7bf31458aba1a6da6d31c5d00

An audio webcast of the conference call will run simultaneously on the company’s website at www.innospec.com. The relevant link as well as the slide presentation for the conference call will be found in the Investor Relations section of the website.

A replay of the webcast can also be accessed from the company’s website and will be available for 30 days following the call.

About Innospec Inc.

Innospec Inc. is an international specialty chemicals company with approximately 2,450 employees in 22 countries. Innospec manufactures and supplies a wide range of specialty chemicals to markets in the Americas, Europe, the Middle East, Africa and Asia-Pacific. The Performance Chemicals business creates innovative technology-based solutions for our customers in the Personal Care, Home Care, Agrochemical, Mining and Industrial markets. The Fuel Specialties business specializes in manufacturing and supplying fuel additives that improve fuel efficiency, boost engine performance and reduce harmful emissions. Oilfield Services provides specialty chemicals to all elements of the oil and gas exploration and production industry. 

Forward-Looking Statements

All statements other than statements of historical facts included or incorporated herein may constitute forward-looking statements. Such forward-looking statements include statements (covered by words like “expects,” “estimates,” “anticipates,” “may,” “could,” “believes,” “feels,” “plans,” “intends,” “outlook” or similar words or expressions, for example) which relate to earnings, growth potential, operating performance, events or developments that we expect or anticipate will or may occur in the future. Although forward-looking statements are believed by management to be reasonable when made, they are subject to certain risks, uncertainties and assumptions, and our actual performance or results may differ materially from these forward-looking statements. Additional information regarding risks, uncertainties and assumptions relating to Innospec and affecting our business operations and prospects are described in Innospec’s Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025, Innospec’s Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the quarter ended September 30, 2025 and other reports filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. data.title: Super Micro's Board Investigates Alleged Smuggling of Nvidia Chips to China
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Super Micro Computer said a group led by members of its board has launched an independent investigation related to the indictment of two former employees and a contractor over an alleged scheme to smuggle high-end Nvidia chips to China.

The server maker said Scott Angel, lead independent director of its board, and Tally Liu, who chairs the board's audit committee, are leading the investigation. Angel and Liu will report their findings to the other four independent members of the board, the company said.

The group has also enlisted the help of law firm Munger, Tolles & Olson, as well as AlixPartners as an independent consultant. The group plans to work with Super Micro's auditor BDO USA.

The independent directors have not set a timeline for the investigation. Super Micro said it would provide an update once the investigation is complete.

The investigation comes amid an alleged scheme to smuggle Nvidia chips to China, which involved billions of dollars of servers and dummy devices used to deceive an American inspector, according to a U.S. indictment.

Super Micro said it no longer has a relationship with the three individuals allegedly involved in the scheme. The company had previously placed its co-founder Yih-Shyan "Wally" Liaw, who was a senior vice president, on leave after learning of his alleged role in the scheme, after which Liaw resigned. Super Micro previously said it also placed a second employee on leave and fired a contractor.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Portofino Announces Share Consolidation
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - April 7, 2026) - PORTOFINO RESOURCES INC. (OTC Pink: PFFOF) (FSE: POTA) ("Portofino" or the "Company") reports that the Company intends to undertake a ten (10) for one (1) share consolidation whereby ten common shares of the Company (the "Common Shares") shall be exchanged for one post-consolidation common share of the Company (the "Post-Consolidation Shares"). The Company currently has approximately 175,443,899 Common Shares issued and outstanding and would have approximately 17,544,389 Post-Consolidation Shares. All outstanding warrants (~45,304,000) and incentive stock options (~11,502,000) would be consolidated on the same basis pursuant to the certificates evidencing such warrants and options.

Portofino's Board of Directors anticipates the share consolidation will give the Company more flexibility to secure less dilutive financing and expand both its current project exploration strategy and project portfolio. The Company's articles authorize the Board of Directors to approve the share consolidation, and the Board has approved the consolidation. No shareholder approval is required. The Company name or stock symbol remain unchanged.

Upon completion of the consolidation and TSX Venture Exchange (the "TSXV") approval, a "Letter of Transmittal" will be mailed to shareholders holding physical certificates by the Company's transfer agent (TMX Equity Transfer Services), advising that the consolidation has taken effect and shareholders should surrender their existing (pre-consolidation) common share certificates, for new (post-consolidation) common share certificates. No fractional common shares of the Company shall be issued in connection with the consolidation and the number of common shares to be received by a Shareholder shall be rounded down to the nearest whole number.

About Portofino Resources Inc.Portofino is a Vancouver, Canada-based company focused on exploring and developing mineral resource projects in the Americas. Portofino holds a 100% interest in the (drill ready) Yergo Lithium Project in Catamarca, Argentina situated in the heart of the world-renowned Argentine Lithium Triangle. The Company also holds a 100% interest in two gold exploration projects located within northwestern Ontario, Canada, including the drill-ready, South of Otter, Red Lake gold project, and the Gold Creek, Thunder Bay project which has been optioned to Delta Resources Limited.

ON BEHALF OF THE BOARD

"Rodney Campbell"

Director, Interim Chief Executive Officer

For Further Information Contact:

604-683-1991

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

data.title: U.S. Oil Prices Rise as Iran Rebuffs Trump's Threat That Whole Civilization 'Will Die Tonight' — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Anita Hamilton and Callum Keown

U.S. oil prices settled higher Tuesday as Iran appeared to reject a temporary ceasefire and President Donald Trump ramped up threats with just hours remaining before his 8 p.m. Eastern time deadline for a peace deal.

A last ditch effort from Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif to postpone the deadline by two weeks caused international oil prices to fall slightly, while U.S. prices continued to rise.

After the U.S. struck military targets on Kharg Island, which handles 90% of Iran's oil exports, Trump wrote on Truth Social that a "whole civilization will die tonight, never to be brought back again."

Iran's ambassador to the United Nations, Amir Saeid Iravani, told the security council that Iran "will never submit to pressure, coercion or force." He also called on the U.N. to demand an end to the attacks.

While West Texas Intermediate oil futures settled up 0.5% at $112.95, Brent crude futures settled down 0.5% to $109.27.

U.S. oil futures are trading higher than the international benchmark because they're based on contracts for delivery in May, meaning they reflect how the market sees oil prices over the next four weeks, said Jaime Brito, executive director of Refining and Oil Products at Oil Price Information Service. Dow Jones is the parent company of both Barron's and OPIS.

By comparison, the price of Brent futures is based on contracts for delivery in June and reflects what the market expects over the next eight weeks.

Another factor keeping a relative lid on Brent prices may be the slight uptick in tankers successfully traversing the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend, according to ship tracking service MarineTraffic.

On Saturday, the number of crossings peaked at 11 — the most in a single day since March 1 — after Iran began allowing Iraqi vessels to move through the waterway. What's more there have been no reported physical attacks on ships there since April 1.

"We continue to see the near-term conflict path clearly pointing to escalation, even if the White House announces another extension," RBC Capital Markets' Helina Croft wrote Tuesday. "The negotiating gap between Tehran and Washington remains wide."

Echoing that sentiment, J.P. Morgan analysts wrote Tuesday that Iran "has limited incentive to fully reopen the Strait without a comprehensive ceasefire agreement with the U.S."

Write to Anita Hamilton at anita.hamilton@barrons.com and Callum Keown at callum.keown@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Venture-Capital Firm Eclipse Raises $1.3 Billion — Market Talk
data.description: Venture-capital firm Eclipse closes on $1.3 billion raised across two funds, bringing the Palo Alto, Calif., firm's total assets under management to about $10 billion. Eclipse focuses on industries such as manufacturing, energy and defense, including the space defense company True Anomaly, the aerospace defense firm Ursa Major and Blue Water Autonomy, which designs autonomous ships. The firm's new funding comes amid a wave of venture-capital investment in U.S. defense-technology startups, as the Pentagon has in recent years grown increasingly amenable to Silicon Valley's pitches. Defense-technology companies have marketed a range of products to the administration, from missile shields to drone technology. (chris.kuo@wsj.com)

data.title: Kura 10-Q: $153.5M Revenue; $(0.39) EPS for Six Months Ended Feb 28, 2026
data.description:
Kura reported $153.5 million in sales for the six months ended Feb 28, 2026, an 18.6% increase year‑over‑year driven by new restaurant openings and menu price increases. The company posted a net loss of $4.8 million, or $(0.39) per Class A and Class B share (basic and diluted), with operating loss of $5.9 million and a modest improvement versus the prior year. Gross profit of $14.9 million and restaurant-level operating profit of $25.7 million reflect ongoing operational investments and pricing actions amid elevated cost pressures.

Financial Highlights

  • Sales: $153.5 million for the six months ended Feb 28, 2026 (up 18.6% YoY).
  • Gross Profit: $14.9 million for the six months ended Feb 28, 2026.
  • Operating loss: $(5.9) million for the six months ended Feb 28, 2026 (operating loss margin 3.9%; improved $0.2 million vs prior year).
  • Net loss: $(4.8) million for the six months ended Feb 28, 2026 (essentially flat YoY); income tax expense $0.09 million.
  • Net loss per Class A and Class B shares: $(0.39) basic and diluted for the six months ended Feb 28, 2026.
  • Restaurant-level operating profit: $25.7 million for the six months ended Feb 28, 2026.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Growth was driven by new restaurant openings and menu price increases, supporting an 18.6% YTD sales increase and a 23.3% increase in the quarter.
  • Unit expansion: Opened 5 restaurants in the six-month period (84 total at period end); opened 4 additional restaurants after period end (88 total); plans call for 16 openings in FY2026.
  • Comparable sales momentum: Comparable sales rose 8.6% in the quarter and 3.0% year‑to‑date, supported by traffic gains and favorable price/mix from promotions and price increases.
  • Cost pressures and pricing response: Tariffs and commodity inflation increased food costs; the company implemented menu price increases to help offset these headwinds.
  • Operational investments: Increased restaurant-level spending on pre-opening costs, remodels and capital expenditures, along with heightened marketing and promotional collaborations to drive traffic.

Original SEC Filing: KURA SUSHI USA, INC. [ KRUS ] - 10-Q - Apr. 07, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Greenbrier 10-Q: Revenue $587.5M, EPS $0.47 for Q (three months ended Feb 28, 2026)
data.description:
Greenbrier reported revenue of $587.5 million for the quarter ended Feb. 28, 2026, a 22.9% decline year‑over‑year, driven by lower manufacturing deliveries; diluted earnings per share were $0.47, down from $1.56 a year earlier. The quarter reflected reduced manufacturing margins and lower consolidated profitability while leasing activity and fleet dispositions provided offsetting benefits. Management highlighted continued focus on recurring lease revenue and backlog fulfillment into 2027 and beyond.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $587.5 million, down 22.9% YoY (three months ended Feb 28, 2026 vs 2025).
  • Gross Profit: $69.5 million; gross margin 11.8% (three months ended Feb 28, 2026).
  • Earnings from operations: $25.1 million (reported as "Earnings from operations").
  • Net earnings (consolidated): $13.9 million; Net earnings attributable to Greenbrier: $15.0 million, down 71.1% YoY (three months ended Feb 28, 2026).
  • Diluted earnings per share: $0.47, decreased from $1.56 in the prior-year quarter (three months ended Feb 28, 2026).

Business Highlights

  • Manufacturing trends: Manufacturing revenue fell roughly 24% with deliveries down about 32%, shifting product mix toward lower‑margin railcar types and reducing manufacturing margins.
  • Channel and product mix: A shift in product mix toward lower‑margin railcar types and fewer deliveries materially impacted margin performance in the manufacturing segment.
  • Leasing and fleet management: Rents from leasing and fleet management increased; lease‑fleet sales rose, contributing higher net gains on dispositions and supporting recurring revenue.
  • Backlog and syndication: Order backlog stood at approximately 15,200 units (~$2.1 billion), which includes about $650 million intended for syndication; scheduled deliveries extend into 2027 and beyond.
  • Operational positioning: The company emphasizes its global footprint and integrated model, and is focusing on expanding recurring lease revenue and managing cyclicality through lease growth and backlog execution.

Original SEC Filing: GREENBRIER COMPANIES INC [ GBX ] - 10-Q - Apr. 07, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Cintas 10-Q: Revenue $2,841.4M, EPS $1.24
data.description:
Cintas reported third-quarter revenue of $2,841.4 million, an 8.9% increase year-over-year driven by organic growth and pricing, and delivered diluted earnings per share of $1.24, up 9.7% from the prior year largely due to higher net income and share repurchases. Net income rose to $502.5 million, reflecting operational improvement and leverage. The company cited improved gross margins from inventory efficiency and sourcing initiatives supporting operating performance.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $2,841.4 million, up 8.9% year-over-year (organic growth ~8.2%).
  • Gross margin: $1,448.5 million, improved as a percentage of revenue due to inventory efficiency and sourcing initiatives.
  • Operating income: $659.9 million (23.2% of revenue); noted improvement excluding a prior-period asset sale gain.
  • Net income: $502.5 million, up 8.4% year-over-year.
  • Diluted earnings per share: $1.24, up 9.7% year-over-year, driven by higher net income and share repurchases.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Growth led by new business, price increases and strong customer retention.
  • Segment momentum: First Aid & Safety delivered approximately 15% growth in the quarter; Uniform & Facility services grew roughly 7–8%.
  • Operational efficiency: Improved in‑service inventory utilization, sourcing initiatives and fixed-cost leverage contributed to margin expansion.
  • Sales execution: Increased penetration of additional products and services into existing customers and expansion of national sales efforts.
  • Capital deployment: Continued strategic acquisitions, ongoing share repurchase program and sustained dividend policy to support long-term strategy.

Original SEC Filing: CINTAS CORP [ CTAS ] - 10-Q - Apr. 07, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: FTSE Russell confirms Vietnam's emerging market status
data.description:
FTSE Russell will upgrade Vietnam to emerging market status from frontier status in September, the index provider said in an interim review on Tuesday, the final seal of approval for the long-awaited move.

Vietnam will be added to FTSE Russell's global equity indices beginning on September 21 in a phased manner which will continue into 2027, the index provider said in a statement.

FTSE Russell had upgraded Vietnam in October to emerging market status from frontier, but that was subject to an interim review to determine whether enough progress had been made by the country in enabling access to global brokers.

The decision puts Vietnam on par with markets like India and China and follows market-friendly reforms by the Communist-ruled Southeast Asian country. It will also allow many passively managed funds to buy shares of locally listed companies.

Vietnam's benchmark stock index is down 6% so far this year as the war in the Middle East has sapped sentiment. It surged about 41% in 2025, its strongest gain in eight years, as the export-reliant nation posted economic growth of 8%.

data.title: Levi Strauss raises annual forecasts on resilient demand for premium jeans
data.description:
Key points:
  • Levi Strauss beating Q1 sales estimates on strong DTC sales
  • Premium denim and direct-to-consumer sales offset U.S. tariff impact
  • Americas, Europe, and Asia sales rise in Q1

Levi Strauss on Tuesday raised its annual sales and profit forecasts after beating first-quarter estimates, as resilient demand for its premium denims and strength in its direct-to-consumer business helped offset a hit from U.S. tariffs.

The jeans maker has been working to blunt the impact of higher U.S. import duties through a mix of price hikes, cost controls, supplier negotiations and a diversified sourcing base that is less reliant on China.

In January, the company said it expected a 150-basis-point hit to fiscal 2026 margins or roughly $100 million, which it planned to fully offset.

Levi now expects fiscal 2026 net revenue growth in the range of 5.5% to 6.5%, compared with its prior forecast of a 5% to 6% rise. Analysts, on average, had expected a growth of 5.7%, according to data compiled by LSEG.

The company raised its forecast for annual adjusted earnings per share to a range of $1.42 to $1.48 from with its prior outlook between $1.40 and $1.46.

Shares of the company were up nearly 6% in extended trading. The stock has risen about 45% over the past 12 months.

The upbeat outlook adds to signs that demand for core denim categories remains resilient, especially at the premium end of the market, despite pressure on budgets of low- and middle-income households.

Its premium denim line Blue Tab, non-denim men's bottoms, and women's denim skirts and dresses had been selling well, finance chief Harmit Singh said at an industry conference earlier this year.

Levi also topped Wall Street estimates for first-quarter sales, driven by a 9% jump in sales in its largest market, the Americas. Europe posted a 24% increase, while Asia sales rose 13%.

Comparable sales at its direct-to-consumer channel, a higher margin segment that includes Levi's website and stores, rose 7% in the quarter ended March 1.

Net revenue rose 14% to $1.74 billion for the quarter, beating analysts' average estimate of $1.65 billion. It posted an adjusted earnings of 42 cents per share, compared with 38 cents a year earlier.

The strong results echo those of Abercrombie & Fitch and Gap , who have also noted in recent quarters that shoppers continue to spend on staple wardrobe items such as jeans.

data.title: Bill Gates Set To Testify Before Congress On Epstein Links
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Bill Gates is going to testify in front of the House Oversight Committee on June 10 about his past connections with Jeffrey Epstein. This comes as lawmakers keep digging into documents tied to the late financier's activities.

A Gates spokesperson mentioned that he plans to fully cooperate and will answer questions to help with the committee's investigation. It's worth noting that Gates hasn't faced accusations of misconduct from Epstein's victims, and officials have indicated that his name appearing in released documents doesn't imply he did anything wrong.

Earlier this year, millions of documents related to Epstein were made available to the public after legislation signed by Donald Trump called for more extensive disclosure of Justice Department materials. Among these records were references to Gates' communications and meetings with Epstein.

Gates has acknowledged in the past that meeting Epstein was a grave mistake and expressed regret about the association. He emphasized that their interactions were limited to dinners and stated he never visited Epstein's island or engaged in any illegal activities.

Other notable figures, like Bill Clinton and Hillary Clinton, have also testified before the committee.
